articles.headline: Government Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Enhanced Production Capacity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to significantly scale up production capacity and reduce costs.
• The new phase focuses on expanding electrolyzer manufacturing, developing green hydrogen hubs, and promoting R&D in storage and transport technologies.
• Financial incentives and regulatory frameworks are being streamlined to attract private investment and foster indigenous manufacturing.
• The mission is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
• It also aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, creating new job opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Development
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling production, R&D, and manufacturing.
Target: 5 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) of green hydrogen production by 2030 (initial target, likely to be revised upwards).
Key components: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme, pilot projects, R&D, skill development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic potential of green hydrogen for India, including job creation and export opportunities.
Analyze the role of government incentives and policy frameworks in driving green hydrogen adoption and investment.
Evaluate the environmental benefits of the mission in achieving India's climate targets and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities in green hydrogen production, storage, and transportation.
Critically assess the strategic importance of the mission for India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Fire Engulfs Temporary Tents at Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, No Injuries Reported
articles.explainer:
• A fire, reportedly caused by an exploding gas cylinder, destroyed at least 18 temporary tents at the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela festival grounds.
• Authorities confirmed no injuries or fatalities, attributing it to rapid response and evacuation protocols.
• The incident highlights the challenges of disaster management and safety protocols in large-scale temporary settlements.
• Investigations are underway to ascertain the exact cause and review safety standards for future arrangements.
• The event underscores the importance of stringent fire safety measures and crowd management during mega-events.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Disaster and Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Kumbh Mela is a major pilgrimage and festival in Hinduism.
Held every 12 years at four sites: Prayagraj, Haridwar, Ujjain, and Nashik.
Recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
Disaster management for such large gatherings involves temporary infrastructure, crowd control, and emergency services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique challenges of disaster management in the context of large-scale religious gatherings like the Kumbh Mela.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current safety protocols and infrastructure planning for temporary cities at such events.
Suggest measures to enhance fire safety, crowd control, and emergency response mechanisms at mega-events in India.
Analyze the role of technology and community participation in mitigating risks during mass congregations.
articles.imageDescription: Aerial view of a temporary tent city at Kumbh Mela with emergency services on site.
articles.trivia: The Kumbh Mela is considered the largest peaceful gathering in the world, with tens of millions of pilgrims participating.

articles.headline: Renowned Malayalam Translator K. K. Gangadharan Passes Away at 75
articles.explainer:
• K. K. Gangadharan, a celebrated Malayalam translator, passed away at the age of 75.
• He was highly regarded for his significant contributions to bridging linguistic gaps by translating numerous works from various languages into Malayalam.
• His works enriched Malayalam literature, introducing readers to diverse global narratives and philosophical texts.
• Gangadharan's demise is mourned by the literary fraternity, recognizing his role in promoting cultural exchange through translation.
• He received several accolades for his literary endeavors throughout his career.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: K. K. Gangadharan was a prominent Malayalam translator.
His work helped spread literature from other languages into Malayalam.
Kerala is known for its rich literary tradition.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of K.K. Gangadharan, an elderly man with glasses.
articles.trivia: Translation plays a crucial role in the development of regional languages by introducing new ideas, styles, and genres.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame inflation, which remains above the comfort zone despite recent moderation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest future rate cuts will depend on a sustained decline in headline inflation and stable global economic conditions.
• The RBI also highlighted global uncertainties and geopolitical tensions as key risks to the domestic inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI appointees).
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Examine the impact of global economic conditions and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Critically assess the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for future interest rate movements and credit growth.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Defence and Space Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on enhancing cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• Key discussions included joint development of military platforms, technology transfer, and collaboration in counter-terrorism efforts.
• Space cooperation saw agreements on satellite navigation, earth observation, and human spaceflight missions.
• The dialogue underscored the growing strategic convergence between India and France as key partners.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France have a long-standing strategic partnership.
Key areas of cooperation: defence, space, nuclear energy, counter-terrorism.
France is a major supplier of defence equipment to India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are committed to the Indo-Pacific strategy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the changing global order.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, with a special focus on defence and space sectors.
Evaluate the role of India-France collaboration in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further strengthening bilateral ties, including technology transfer and joint ventures.
Critically assess how this partnership contributes to India's strategic autonomy and diversification of its foreign relations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French flags displayed side by side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications, including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The satellite features enhanced spectral bands and improved spatial resolution compared to its predecessors.
• This mission further bolsters India's capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to national development programs.
• The launch also carried several co-passenger payloads, including student satellites, demonstrating ISRO's commitment to fostering space education.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
EOS series satellites are designed for earth observation.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle.
Applications of EOS data: agriculture, urban planning, disaster monitoring, water resource management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and security.
Analyze the advancements in remote sensing technology demonstrated by EOS-08 and its potential applications.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting indigenous space capabilities and its contributions to global space exploration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in commercializing space technology and services in India.
Critically assess how space technology can aid in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad, carrying the EOS-08 satellite.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Ambit of 'Right to be Forgotten' in Digital Age
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, provided clarity on the 'Right to be Forgotten' in the context of India's digital landscape.
• The judgment emphasized the need to balance an individual's right to privacy with the public's right to information and freedom of expression.
• It outlined specific criteria under which personal information can be de-indexed or removed from public search results, particularly concerning sensitive personal data and past criminal records.
• The ruling acknowledges the challenges posed by pervasive digital footprints and aims to provide recourse for individuals seeking to control their online presence.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for data protection laws and online content regulation in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is a facet of the Right to Privacy, which is a fundamental right under Article 21.
It allows individuals to request removal of personal data from public platforms, especially after a certain period or if it's no longer relevant.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
India's Data Protection Act (if enacted by 2025) likely includes provisions for this right.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis and legal evolution of the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
Analyze the complexities involved in balancing the 'Right to be Forgotten' with other fundamental rights like freedom of speech and right to information.
Evaluate the practical challenges in implementing this right, particularly for internet intermediaries and search engines.
Examine the potential impact of this ruling on data protection frameworks, journalistic freedom, and public accountability.
Critically assess how this judgment contributes to strengthening digital rights and individual autonomy in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a digital footprint being erased or faded out.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' originated in the European Union with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Boost Millet Production and Export
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a comprehensive initiative to promote millet cultivation, processing, and export.
• The program aims to capitalize on the 'International Year of Millets 2023' momentum and enhance farmer incomes.
• Key components include providing financial assistance for millet farming, developing value chains, and promoting millet-based products.
• Efforts will also focus on market linkages, branding, and export promotion to position India as a global leader in millet production.
• This initiative aligns with India's nutritional security goals and sustainable agriculture practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India championed the 'International Year of Millets 2023' at the UN.
Millets are nutri-cereals, drought-resistant, and require less water.
Examples: Jowar (Sorghum), Bajra (Pearl Millet), Ragi (Finger Millet).
India is one of the largest producers of millets globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the nutritional and ecological benefits of millets and their role in ensuring India's food and nutritional security.
Analyze the challenges faced by millet farmers and the strategies adopted by the government to boost millet production and value addition.
Evaluate the potential of millets to diversify India's agricultural exports and contribute to farmer incomes.
Examine the role of public awareness and market demand in driving the revival of millet consumption in India.
Critically assess how the new initiative aligns with sustainable agriculture practices and climate resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A variety of millets displayed in bowls, with a farmer in the background.
articles.trivia: Millets are often referred to as 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional content, including protein, fiber, and essential minerals.

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Achieves 75% Rural Household Tap Water Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced that the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) has achieved 75% tap water connectivity to rural households across India.
• This significant milestone brings the mission closer to its goal of providing functional household tap connections (FHTC) to every rural home by 2024.
• The mission has focused on community participation, local ownership, and technological solutions for water quality monitoring.
• States like Goa, Telangana, Haryana, Gujarat, and Punjab have already achieved 100% saturation.
• The success of JJM is crucial for improving public health, reducing drudgery for women, and promoting rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission launched in August 2019.
Aim: To provide FHTC to every rural household by 2024.
Implemented by the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus on 'Har Ghar Jal' (water to every home).
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key strategies employed by the Jal Jeevan Mission to achieve universal tap water connectivity in rural areas.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of improved water access on public health, women's empowerment, and rural livelihoods.
Evaluate the challenges faced in the implementation of JJM, including water quality, sustainability of sources, and regional disparities.
Examine the role of technology, community participation, and inter-state cooperation in the success of such large-scale welfare schemes.
Critically assess how JJM contributes to India's broader development goals and its alignment with Sustainable Development Goal 6 (Clean Water and Sanitation).
articles.imageDescription: A rural woman filling water from a tap connection in her home.
articles.trivia: The Jal Jeevan Mission is one of the largest government-led initiatives globally aiming for universal access to safe drinking water.

articles.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Renewable Energy Technologies and Investment
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting a major International Conference on Renewable Energy Technologies and Investment, attracting delegates from over 50 countries.
• The conference aims to foster global collaboration on accelerating the deployment of renewable energy technologies and mobilize green finance.
• Discussions are centered on advancements in solar, wind, green hydrogen, and battery storage technologies.
• India showcased its progress in renewable energy capacity addition and its ambitious targets for energy transition.
• The event serves as a platform for knowledge sharing, policy dialogue, and forging partnerships to combat climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Energy
prelimsPoints: India is a leading country in renewable energy capacity addition.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
Targets include 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Green finance refers to financial investments flowing into sustainable development projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in promoting renewable energy and its commitment to global climate action.
Analyze the key technological advancements and investment opportunities highlighted at the conference for accelerating energy transition.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing green finance and facilitating technology transfer to developing nations for renewable energy deployment.
Examine the policy measures and regulatory frameworks required to create an enabling environment for renewable energy growth in India.
Critically assess the importance of international cooperation and multilateral platforms in achieving global climate goals and sustainable energy access.
articles.imageDescription: A conference hall with a panel discussion on renewable energy, featuring large screens showing solar panels and wind turbines.
articles.trivia: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is the fourth largest in the world.

articles.headline: Border Security Force Detects Drone Intrusion Along Punjab Border, Recovers Contraband
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) detected and intercepted a drone intrusion attempt along the international border in Punjab.
• Following a search operation, BSF personnel recovered narcotics and a weapon package dropped by the drone.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenge of cross-border smuggling and the evolving tactics used by illicit networks.
• BSF has intensified surveillance and deployed advanced anti-drone technologies to counter such threats.
• The use of drones for smuggling poses significant internal security challenges, particularly in border states.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Punjab shares an international border with Pakistan.
Drones are increasingly used for smuggling arms, narcotics, and counterfeit currency.
Anti-drone technology includes jammers, net guns, and laser-based systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cross-border security threats, with a focus on drone technology, along India's western borders.
Analyze the challenges faced by border guarding forces like BSF in countering drone intrusions and illicit activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current technological solutions and operational strategies employed to enhance border surveillance and security.
Examine the linkages between cross-border smuggling, organized crime, and internal security challenges in states like Punjab.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including policy interventions and international cooperation, to mitigate drone-related threats to national security.
articles.imageDescription: BSF personnel inspecting a recovered drone and contraband items near a border fence.
articles.trivia: BSF is also deployed for anti-Naxal operations and internal security duties in addition to border guarding.

articles.headline: Discussion on Uniform Civil Code Gains Momentum in Specific States
articles.explainer:
• The debate around the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) has seen renewed momentum, particularly in certain states proposing its adoption.
• Proponents argue that a UCC would promote gender justice, national integration, and simplify personal laws currently governed by diverse religious customs.
• Opponents raise concerns about religious freedom, cultural diversity, and potential implications for minority communities.
• The Law Commission of India has previously sought public opinion on the matter, indicating ongoing deliberations at the national level.
• The discussion highlights the complex interplay between constitutional principles, secularism, and cultural identity in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: UCC is mentioned in Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
Aims to replace personal laws based on religious scriptures and customs with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, adoption, etc.
Part IV of the Constitution deals with DPSP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate for a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) and its historical context in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and religious freedom.
Evaluate the challenges in formulating and implementing a UCC that respects India's diverse cultural and religious landscape.
Examine the potential socio-political implications of introducing a UCC, particularly for minority communities.
Critically assess how a UCC could be harmonized with the principles of secularism and pluralism enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
articles.imageDescription: Scales of justice balancing different religious symbols against a single civil code book.
articles.trivia: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was a strong proponent of the Uniform Civil Code, believing it essential for national unity.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft Policy Framework for AI Governance in India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a draft policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance in India, inviting public comments.
• The framework aims to promote responsible AI development, ensuring ethical deployment, data privacy, and accountability.
• It addresses key aspects such as data quality, algorithmic bias, transparency, and the socio-economic impact of AI.
• India seeks to harness AI's potential for economic growth and public service delivery while mitigating associated risks.
• This policy is crucial for positioning India as a global leader in AI innovation with a focus on 'AI for All'.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.
AI governance refers to the policies and frameworks for developing and deploying AI systems responsibly.
Key concerns in AI: bias, privacy, security, transparency, accountability.
India aims to leverage AI in sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind developing a comprehensive AI governance framework for India, considering its unique socio-economic context.
Analyze the key principles and challenges addressed in NITI Aayog's draft policy, such as algorithmic bias, data privacy, and ethical considerations.
Evaluate the potential impact of AI on employment, economic growth, and public service delivery in India.
Examine the role of government, industry, and civil society in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
Critically assess how India can position itself as a global leader in AI innovation while upholding ethical standards and promoting 'AI for All'.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected nodes representing artificial intelligence and governance concepts.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.

articles.headline: IMF Global Economic Outlook: India's Growth Projection Revised Upwards
articles.explainer:
• The International Monetary Fund (IMF) released its latest 'World Economic Outlook' report, revising India's growth projection upwards for the current fiscal year.
• The revision is attributed to robust domestic demand, strong public investment, and a resilient services sector.
• Despite global headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties, India is expected to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies.
• The report, however, cautioned about persistent inflation risks and the need for continued structural reforms.
• This positive outlook reinforces confidence in India's economic fundamentals and its potential to drive global growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effect of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: IMF is an international organization, headquartered in Washington, D.C., working to foster global monetary cooperation.
It publishes the 'World Economic Outlook' report periodically.
GDP (Gross Domestic Product) is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
Structural reforms aim to improve the functioning of the economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's upward revision in the IMF's growth projections, despite global economic challenges.
Discuss the implications of a strong Indian economy for global economic stability and trade.
Evaluate the persistent risks highlighted by the IMF, such as inflation and global geopolitical tensions, and their potential impact on India's growth trajectory.
Examine the role of government policies and structural reforms in sustaining India's economic momentum.
Critically assess India's potential to become a major engine of global growth and the challenges it needs to overcome.
articles.imageDescription: IMF logo against a backdrop of global economic charts and graphs.
articles.trivia: India is a founding member of the International Monetary Fund.

articles.headline: Latest Forest Survey Report Highlights Increase in India's Mangrove Cover
articles.explainer:
• The biennial India State of Forest Report (ISFR) indicates a significant increase in India's mangrove cover over the past two years.
• The report attributes this growth to sustained conservation efforts, afforestation programs, and community participation.
• Mangroves play a crucial role in protecting coastlines, supporting biodiversity, and acting as carbon sinks.
• West Bengal, Gujarat, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands continue to hold the largest mangrove areas.
• This positive trend underscores India's commitment to coastal ecosystem protection and climate resilience.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: ISFR is published biennially by the Forest Survey of India (FSI).
Mangroves are salt-tolerant plant communities found in tropical and subtropical intertidal zones.
They protect coastlines from erosion, storm surges, and tsunamis.
Sundarbans in West Bengal is the largest mangrove forest in the world.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of mangroves and their role in coastal protection and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increase in India's mangrove cover, including government policies and community initiatives.
Evaluate the challenges faced in mangrove conservation, such as encroachment, pollution, and climate change impacts.
Examine the socio-economic benefits derived from healthy mangrove ecosystems for local communities.
Critically assess India's strategies for coastal zone management and the importance of integrated approaches for mangrove protection.
articles.imageDescription: Dense mangrove forest along a calm coastal waterway.
articles.trivia: Mangroves are often called 'coastal sentinels' due to their role in protecting shorelines.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Restoration of Ancient Temple Site in Karnataka
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a major restoration project at an ancient temple complex in Hampi, Karnataka.
• The project focuses on preserving the structural integrity and intricate carvings of the Vijayanagara-era temple.
• Advanced techniques, including laser scanning and chemical conservation, are being employed to ensure authenticity and longevity.
• This initiative is part of a broader effort to protect and promote India's rich cultural heritage and historical sites.
• The restoration aims to enhance the visitor experience while safeguarding the historical significance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Hampi is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire.
Vijayanagara Empire (14th-17th centuries) was a prominent South Indian empire.
Karnataka is rich in historical sites and temple architecture (e.g., Hoysala, Chalukya, Vijayanagara).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving ancient architectural sites for understanding India's cultural heritage and historical continuity.
Analyze the role of institutions like the Archaeological Survey of India in the conservation and management of historical monuments.
Evaluate the challenges involved in restoring ancient structures, including balancing preservation with accessibility and tourism.
Examine the economic and cultural benefits of promoting heritage tourism in India.
Critically assess the modern techniques and scientific approaches used in archaeological conservation and their effectiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Ancient stone temple ruins at Hampi, with scaffolding for restoration work.
articles.trivia: The Virupaksha Temple in Hampi has been continuously functioning since the 7th century AD.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Fisheries Talks Resume Amidst Persistent Disputes
articles.explainer:
• High-level fisheries talks between India and Sri Lanka resumed, aiming to resolve the long-standing dispute over fishing rights in the Palk Strait.
• The discussions focused on issues such as bottom trawling, territorial waters, and the welfare of detained fishermen.
• Both sides expressed commitment to finding a pragmatic and humane solution, recognizing the livelihood concerns of fishing communities.
• The dispute frequently leads to arrests of Indian fishermen by Sri Lankan authorities and confiscation of their boats.
• Diplomacy and joint patrolling mechanisms are being explored to de-escalate tensions and ensure sustainable fishing practices.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka.
Kachatheevu Island was ceded to Sri Lanka by India in 1974.
Bottom trawling is a destructive fishing method banned in Sri Lanka.
Tamil Nadu fishermen are often involved in these disputes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of the dispute for fishing communities on both sides of the border.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various diplomatic and technical solutions proposed to resolve the issue, including joint patrolling and alternative livelihood options.
Examine the environmental concerns associated with unsustainable fishing practices like bottom trawling and their impact on marine ecosystems.
Critically assess how this persistent dispute affects the broader bilateral relations between India and Sri Lanka.
articles.imageDescription: Fishing boats in the Palk Strait, with Indian and Sri Lankan coastlines visible in the distance.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait is relatively shallow and narrow, making it rich in marine resources but also a flashpoint for disputes.

articles.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II Progress: Focus on ODF Plus Status
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II, highlighting advancements towards ODF Plus status.
• ODF Plus aims for not just open defecation free status, but also includes solid and liquid waste management, and visual cleanliness.
• The mission emphasizes sustainable sanitation practices, behavior change, and community involvement in rural areas.
• Significant strides have been made in managing plastic waste, greywater, and fecal sludge through various technological and social interventions.
• SBM-G Phase II is crucial for achieving holistic sanitation and improving the overall quality of life in rural India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: SBM-G launched in 2014, Phase II began in 2020-21.
ODF status means no open defecation.
ODF Plus includes ODF sustainability, solid and liquid waste management.
Ministry of Jal Shakti oversees SBM-G.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Swachh Bharat Mission from ODF to ODF Plus and its expanded objectives in Phase II.
Analyze the challenges and successes in implementing solid and liquid waste management solutions in rural India.
Evaluate the socio-economic and public health benefits of achieving ODF Plus status in rural communities.
Examine the role of behavior change communication, community participation, and technological innovations in sustainable sanitation.
Critically assess the sustainability of infrastructure created under SBM-G and the institutional mechanisms for its long-term maintenance.
articles.imageDescription: A clean rural village scene with waste management infrastructure and smiling villagers.
articles.trivia: SBM-G is one of the world's largest behavior change programs.

highlights: Government launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting enhanced production and R&D for energy security.
A fire caused by an exploding gas cylinder destroys 18 temporary tents at Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela; no injuries reported.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% amidst persistent inflation concerns, indicating a cautious monetary policy stance.
Supreme Court clarifies the ambit of 'Right to be Forgotten' in the digital age, balancing privacy with public information.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08, bolstering India's remote sensing capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The 'Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT)' programme is a key component of the mission.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen, and the SIGHT programme is indeed a crucial part of it. Green hydrogen is defined by its production method: electrolysis of water powered by renewable energy sources, ensuring a near-zero carbon footprint.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India is/are correct?
1. It is explicitly enshrined as a fundamental right under Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. The Supreme Court's 'Puttaswamy judgment' on the Right to Privacy forms its foundational basis.
3. It allows individuals to request the removal of personal data from public platforms under certain conditions.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The 'Right to be Forgotten' is considered a facet of the Right to Privacy, which is implicitly guaranteed under Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty), not explicitly under Article 19. Statement 2 is correct, as the Puttaswamy judgment declared privacy as a fundamental right, providing the basis. Statement 3 is correct, as the right allows for the de-indexing or removal of personal information under certain circumstances.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM):
1. Its primary objective is to provide functional household tap connections (FHTC) to all rural households by 2024.
2. It falls under the purview of the Ministry of Rural Development.
3. Community participation is a key feature of its implementation strategy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the mission aims for FHTC to every rural household by 2024. Statement 2 is incorrect; JJM is implemented by the Ministry of Jal Shakti. Statement 3 is correct, as community participation and local ownership are crucial aspects of the mission's strategy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are the key function(s) of the Border Security Force (BSF) in India?
1. Guarding India's land borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.
2. Engaging in anti-Naxal operations in certain regions.
3. Providing security for sensitive government installations.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. BSF is the primary border guarding force for India's borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh. It is also deployed for internal security duties, including anti-Naxal operations, and provides security for various sensitive installations.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the International Year of Millets (IYM) 2023, consider the following statements:
1. The United Nations declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets at the behest of India.
2. Millets are known for being drought-resistant and having high nutritional value.
3. India is the largest producer of millets globally.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India spearheaded the proposal for IYM 2023 at the UN. Millets are indeed recognized for their climate resilience and nutritional benefits. India is one of the largest producers of millets globally, playing a significant role in their cultivation and promotion.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multifaceted impact of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission on its energy security, climate targets, and economic growth. What are the key challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and what policy measures can address them?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Economic Development; Environmental Pollution & Degradation
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission's objectives. Discuss its benefits for energy security (reduced import dependence), climate (decarbonization, Net Zero), and economy (job creation, new industries, export potential). Identify challenges like high production costs, technology gaps, infrastructure development, and water availability. Suggest policy measures: R&D incentives, PLI schemes, green finance, regulatory clarity, international collaboration, skill development.

mainsMocks.question: The 'Right to be Forgotten' represents a significant evolution in privacy jurisprudence in the digital age. Discuss its implications for individual autonomy and information control, while also examining the challenges in balancing it with the public's right to know and freedom of expression.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Define 'Right to be Forgotten' as a facet of Article 21. Explain its importance for individual autonomy, reputation, and control over digital footprint. Then, present the counter-arguments: freedom of speech (Article 19), public interest in information, historical accuracy, and challenges for internet intermediaries. Conclude with a balanced approach to implementation, considering proportionality and specific criteria.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress, the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait remains a persistent irritant in bilateral relations. Critically examine the underlying causes of this conflict and suggest comprehensive diplomatic and sustainable solutions to foster long-term cooperation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Palk Strait dispute and its historical context (Kachatheevu). Analyze underlying causes: traditional fishing rights, bottom trawling, depletion of marine resources, IMBL, livelihood concerns. Discuss impacts on bilateral relations. Suggest solutions: joint patrolling, alternative livelihoods, deep-sea fishing promotion, technology transfer, diplomatic dialogue, review of existing agreements, community-level engagement, and sustainable fishing practices.

quickBytes.text: The Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela is held every 12 years at the confluence of Ganga, Yamuna, and mythical Saraswati rivers.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI consists of six members, with the RBI Governor as ex-officio Chairperson.
India and France have a strategic partnership established in 1998, covering defence, space, and nuclear energy.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle for Earth Observation Satellites.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution, part of DPSP, mandates the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for citizens.
Millets are often called 'nutri-cereals' due to their rich content of protein, fiber, and micronutrients.
The NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India, established in 2015, replacing the Planning Commission.
Mangroves are vital coastal ecosystems, acting as natural barriers against storms and tsunamis.
Hampi, a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Karnataka, was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) releases the 'World Economic Outlook' report twice a year.

GS1.headline: Fire Engulfs Temporary Tents at Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela, No Injuries Reported
GS1.explainer:
• A fire, reportedly caused by an exploding gas cylinder, destroyed at least 18 temporary tents at the 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela festival grounds.
• Authorities confirmed no injuries or fatalities, attributing it to rapid response and evacuation protocols.
• The incident highlights the challenges of disaster management and safety protocols in large-scale temporary settlements.
• Investigations are underway to ascertain the exact cause and review safety standards for future arrangements.
• The event underscores the importance of stringent fire safety measures and crowd management during mega-events.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Disaster and Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Kumbh Mela is a major pilgrimage and festival in Hinduism.
Held every 12 years at four sites: Prayagraj, Haridwar, Ujjain, and Nashik.
Recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
Disaster management for such large gatherings involves temporary infrastructure, crowd control, and emergency services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique challenges of disaster management in the context of large-scale religious gatherings like the Kumbh Mela.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current safety protocols and infrastructure planning for temporary cities at such events.
Suggest measures to enhance fire safety, crowd control, and emergency response mechanisms at mega-events in India.
Analyze the role of technology and community participation in mitigating risks during mass congregations.
GS1.imageDescription: Aerial view of a temporary tent city at Kumbh Mela with emergency services on site.
GS1.trivia: The Kumbh Mela is considered the largest peaceful gathering in the world, with tens of millions of pilgrims participating.

GS1.headline: Renowned Malayalam Translator K. K. Gangadharan Passes Away at 75
GS1.explainer:
• K. K. Gangadharan, a celebrated Malayalam translator, passed away at the age of 75.
• He was highly regarded for his significant contributions to bridging linguistic gaps by translating numerous works from various languages into Malayalam.
• His works enriched Malayalam literature, introducing readers to diverse global narratives and philosophical texts.
• Gangadharan's demise is mourned by the literary fraternity, recognizing his role in promoting cultural exchange through translation.
• He received several accolades for his literary endeavors throughout his career.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: K. K. Gangadharan was a prominent Malayalam translator.
His work helped spread literature from other languages into Malayalam.
Kerala is known for its rich literary tradition.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of K.K. Gangadharan, an elderly man with glasses.
GS1.trivia: Translation plays a crucial role in the development of regional languages by introducing new ideas, styles, and genres.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Initiates Restoration of Ancient Temple Site in Karnataka
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has commenced a major restoration project at an ancient temple complex in Hampi, Karnataka.
• The project focuses on preserving the structural integrity and intricate carvings of the Vijayanagara-era temple.
• Advanced techniques, including laser scanning and chemical conservation, are being employed to ensure authenticity and longevity.
• This initiative is part of a broader effort to protect and promote India's rich cultural heritage and historical sites.
• The restoration aims to enhance the visitor experience while safeguarding the historical significance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Hampi is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire.
Vijayanagara Empire (14th-17th centuries) was a prominent South Indian empire.
Karnataka is rich in historical sites and temple architecture (e.g., Hoysala, Chalukya, Vijayanagara).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving ancient architectural sites for understanding India's cultural heritage and historical continuity.
Analyze the role of institutions like the Archaeological Survey of India in the conservation and management of historical monuments.
Evaluate the challenges involved in restoring ancient structures, including balancing preservation with accessibility and tourism.
Examine the economic and cultural benefits of promoting heritage tourism in India.
Critically assess the modern techniques and scientific approaches used in archaeological conservation and their effectiveness.
GS1.imageDescription: Ancient stone temple ruins at Hampi, with scaffolding for restoration work.
GS1.trivia: The Virupaksha Temple in Hampi has been continuously functioning since the 7th century AD.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Defence and Space Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on enhancing cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• Key discussions included joint development of military platforms, technology transfer, and collaboration in counter-terrorism efforts.
• Space cooperation saw agreements on satellite navigation, earth observation, and human spaceflight missions.
• The dialogue underscored the growing strategic convergence between India and France as key partners.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France have a long-standing strategic partnership.
Key areas of cooperation: defence, space, nuclear energy, counter-terrorism.
France is a major supplier of defence equipment to India (e.g., Rafale jets, Scorpene submarines).
Both countries are committed to the Indo-Pacific strategy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the changing global order.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, with a special focus on defence and space sectors.
Evaluate the role of India-France collaboration in promoting stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for further strengthening bilateral ties, including technology transfer and joint ventures.
Critically assess how this partnership contributes to India's strategic autonomy and diversification of its foreign relations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and French flags displayed side by side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Ambit of 'Right to be Forgotten' in Digital Age
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, provided clarity on the 'Right to be Forgotten' in the context of India's digital landscape.
• The judgment emphasized the need to balance an individual's right to privacy with the public's right to information and freedom of expression.
• It outlined specific criteria under which personal information can be de-indexed or removed from public search results, particularly concerning sensitive personal data and past criminal records.
• The ruling acknowledges the challenges posed by pervasive digital footprints and aims to provide recourse for individuals seeking to control their online presence.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for data protection laws and online content regulation in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is a facet of the Right to Privacy, which is a fundamental right under Article 21.
It allows individuals to request removal of personal data from public platforms, especially after a certain period or if it's no longer relevant.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
India's Data Protection Act (if enacted by 2025) likely includes provisions for this right.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional basis and legal evolution of the 'Right to be Forgotten' in India.
Analyze the complexities involved in balancing the 'Right to be Forgotten' with other fundamental rights like freedom of speech and right to information.
Evaluate the practical challenges in implementing this right, particularly for internet intermediaries and search engines.
Examine the potential impact of this ruling on data protection frameworks, journalistic freedom, and public accountability.
Critically assess how this judgment contributes to strengthening digital rights and individual autonomy in the digital age.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a digital footprint being erased or faded out.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' originated in the European Union with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

GS2.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission Achieves 75% Rural Household Tap Water Connectivity
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti announced that the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) has achieved 75% tap water connectivity to rural households across India.
• This significant milestone brings the mission closer to its goal of providing functional household tap connections (FHTC) to every rural home by 2024.
• The mission has focused on community participation, local ownership, and technological solutions for water quality monitoring.
• States like Goa, Telangana, Haryana, Gujarat, and Punjab have already achieved 100% saturation.
• The success of JJM is crucial for improving public health, reducing drudgery for women, and promoting rural development.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Jal Jeevan Mission launched in August 2019.
Aim: To provide FHTC to every rural household by 2024.
Implemented by the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus on 'Har Ghar Jal' (water to every home).
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key strategies employed by the Jal Jeevan Mission to achieve universal tap water connectivity in rural areas.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of improved water access on public health, women's empowerment, and rural livelihoods.
Evaluate the challenges faced in the implementation of JJM, including water quality, sustainability of sources, and regional disparities.
Examine the role of technology, community participation, and inter-state cooperation in the success of such large-scale welfare schemes.
Critically assess how JJM contributes to India's broader development goals and its alignment with Sustainable Development Goal 6 (Clean Water and Sanitation).
GS2.imageDescription: A rural woman filling water from a tap connection in her home.
GS2.trivia: The Jal Jeevan Mission is one of the largest government-led initiatives globally aiming for universal access to safe drinking water.

GS2.headline: Discussion on Uniform Civil Code Gains Momentum in Specific States
GS2.explainer:
• The debate around the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) has seen renewed momentum, particularly in certain states proposing its adoption.
• Proponents argue that a UCC would promote gender justice, national integration, and simplify personal laws currently governed by diverse religious customs.
• Opponents raise concerns about religious freedom, cultural diversity, and potential implications for minority communities.
• The Law Commission of India has previously sought public opinion on the matter, indicating ongoing deliberations at the national level.
• The discussion highlights the complex interplay between constitutional principles, secularism, and cultural identity in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: UCC is mentioned in Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy.
Goa is currently the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
Aims to replace personal laws based on religious scriptures and customs with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, adoption, etc.
Part IV of the Constitution deals with DPSP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate for a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) and its historical context in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, considering aspects of gender justice, national integration, and religious freedom.
Evaluate the challenges in formulating and implementing a UCC that respects India's diverse cultural and religious landscape.
Examine the potential socio-political implications of introducing a UCC, particularly for minority communities.
Critically assess how a UCC could be harmonized with the principles of secularism and pluralism enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
GS2.imageDescription: Scales of justice balancing different religious symbols against a single civil code book.
GS2.trivia: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was a strong proponent of the Uniform Civil Code, believing it essential for national unity.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Fisheries Talks Resume Amidst Persistent Disputes
GS2.explainer:
• High-level fisheries talks between India and Sri Lanka resumed, aiming to resolve the long-standing dispute over fishing rights in the Palk Strait.
• The discussions focused on issues such as bottom trawling, territorial waters, and the welfare of detained fishermen.
• Both sides expressed commitment to finding a pragmatic and humane solution, recognizing the livelihood concerns of fishing communities.
• The dispute frequently leads to arrests of Indian fishermen by Sri Lankan authorities and confiscation of their boats.
• Diplomacy and joint patrolling mechanisms are being explored to de-escalate tensions and ensure sustainable fishing practices.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka.
Kachatheevu Island was ceded to Sri Lanka by India in 1974.
Bottom trawling is a destructive fishing method banned in Sri Lanka.
Tamil Nadu fishermen are often involved in these disputes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute in the Palk Strait.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of the dispute for fishing communities on both sides of the border.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various diplomatic and technical solutions proposed to resolve the issue, including joint patrolling and alternative livelihood options.
Examine the environmental concerns associated with unsustainable fishing practices like bottom trawling and their impact on marine ecosystems.
Critically assess how this persistent dispute affects the broader bilateral relations between India and Sri Lanka.
GS2.imageDescription: Fishing boats in the Palk Strait, with Indian and Sri Lankan coastlines visible in the distance.
GS2.trivia: The Palk Strait is relatively shallow and narrow, making it rich in marine resources but also a flashpoint for disputes.

GS2.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II Progress: Focus on ODF Plus Status
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) Phase II, highlighting advancements towards ODF Plus status.
• ODF Plus aims for not just open defecation free status, but also includes solid and liquid waste management, and visual cleanliness.
• The mission emphasizes sustainable sanitation practices, behavior change, and community involvement in rural areas.
• Significant strides have been made in managing plastic waste, greywater, and fecal sludge through various technological and social interventions.
• SBM-G Phase II is crucial for achieving holistic sanitation and improving the overall quality of life in rural India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: SBM-G launched in 2014, Phase II began in 2020-21.
ODF status means no open defecation.
ODF Plus includes ODF sustainability, solid and liquid waste management.
Ministry of Jal Shakti oversees SBM-G.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the Swachh Bharat Mission from ODF to ODF Plus and its expanded objectives in Phase II.
Analyze the challenges and successes in implementing solid and liquid waste management solutions in rural India.
Evaluate the socio-economic and public health benefits of achieving ODF Plus status in rural communities.
Examine the role of behavior change communication, community participation, and technological innovations in sustainable sanitation.
Critically assess the sustainability of infrastructure created under SBM-G and the institutional mechanisms for its long-term maintenance.
GS2.imageDescription: A clean rural village scene with waste management infrastructure and smiling villagers.
GS2.trivia: SBM-G is one of the world's largest behavior change programs.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Enhanced Production Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to significantly scale up production capacity and reduce costs.
• The new phase focuses on expanding electrolyzer manufacturing, developing green hydrogen hubs, and promoting R&D in storage and transport technologies.
• Financial incentives and regulatory frameworks are being streamlined to attract private investment and foster indigenous manufacturing.
• The mission is crucial for India's energy security, decarbonization goals, and achieving Net Zero emissions by 2070.
• It also aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, creating new job opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Development
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling production, R&D, and manufacturing.
Target: 5 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) of green hydrogen production by 2030 (initial target, likely to be revised upwards).
Key components: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme, pilot projects, R&D, skill development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic potential of green hydrogen for India, including job creation and export opportunities.
Analyze the role of government incentives and policy frameworks in driving green hydrogen adoption and investment.
Evaluate the environmental benefits of the mission in achieving India's climate targets and reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
Examine the technological challenges and opportunities in green hydrogen production, storage, and transportation.
Critically assess the strategic importance of the mission for India's energy security and geopolitical standing.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame inflation, which remains above the comfort zone despite recent moderation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest future rate cuts will depend on a sustained decline in headline inflation and stable global economic conditions.
• The RBI also highlighted global uncertainties and geopolitical tensions as key risks to the domestic inflation outlook.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI appointees).
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Examine the impact of global economic conditions and geopolitical events on India's monetary policy decisions.
Critically assess the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for future interest rate movements and credit growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with its logo.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications, including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The satellite features enhanced spectral bands and improved spatial resolution compared to its predecessors.
• This mission further bolsters India's capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to national development programs.
• The launch also carried several co-passenger payloads, including student satellites, demonstrating ISRO's commitment to fostering space education.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
EOS series satellites are designed for earth observation.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse launch vehicle.
Applications of EOS data: agriculture, urban planning, disaster monitoring, water resource management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and security.
Analyze the advancements in remote sensing technology demonstrated by EOS-08 and its potential applications.
Evaluate ISRO's role in promoting indigenous space capabilities and its contributions to global space exploration.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in commercializing space technology and services in India.
Critically assess how space technology can aid in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad, carrying the EOS-08 satellite.
GS3.trivia: India has one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Boost Millet Production and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a comprehensive initiative to promote millet cultivation, processing, and export.
• The program aims to capitalize on the 'International Year of Millets 2023' momentum and enhance farmer incomes.
• Key components include providing financial assistance for millet farming, developing value chains, and promoting millet-based products.
• Efforts will also focus on market linkages, branding, and export promotion to position India as a global leader in millet production.
• This initiative aligns with India's nutritional security goals and sustainable agriculture practices.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India championed the 'International Year of Millets 2023' at the UN.
Millets are nutri-cereals, drought-resistant, and require less water.
Examples: Jowar (Sorghum), Bajra (Pearl Millet), Ragi (Finger Millet).
India is one of the largest producers of millets globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the nutritional and ecological benefits of millets and their role in ensuring India's food and nutritional security.
Analyze the challenges faced by millet farmers and the strategies adopted by the government to boost millet production and value addition.
Evaluate the potential of millets to diversify India's agricultural exports and contribute to farmer incomes.
Examine the role of public awareness and market demand in driving the revival of millet consumption in India.
Critically assess how the new initiative aligns with sustainable agriculture practices and climate resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A variety of millets displayed in bowls, with a farmer in the background.
GS3.trivia: Millets are often referred to as 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional content, including protein, fiber, and essential minerals.

GS3.headline: India Hosts International Conference on Renewable Energy Technologies and Investment
GS3.explainer:
• India is hosting a major International Conference on Renewable Energy Technologies and Investment, attracting delegates from over 50 countries.
• The conference aims to foster global collaboration on accelerating the deployment of renewable energy technologies and mobilize green finance.
• Discussions are centered on advancements in solar, wind, green hydrogen, and battery storage technologies.
• India showcased its progress in renewable energy capacity addition and its ambitious targets for energy transition.
• The event serves as a platform for knowledge sharing, policy dialogue, and forging partnerships to combat climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Energy
prelimsPoints: India is a leading country in renewable energy capacity addition.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India and France.
Targets include 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Green finance refers to financial investments flowing into sustainable development projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in promoting renewable energy and its commitment to global climate action.
Analyze the key technological advancements and investment opportunities highlighted at the conference for accelerating energy transition.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing green finance and facilitating technology transfer to developing nations for renewable energy deployment.
Examine the policy measures and regulatory frameworks required to create an enabling environment for renewable energy growth in India.
Critically assess the importance of international cooperation and multilateral platforms in achieving global climate goals and sustainable energy access.
GS3.imageDescription: A conference hall with a panel discussion on renewable energy, featuring large screens showing solar panels and wind turbines.
GS3.trivia: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is the fourth largest in the world.

GS3.headline: Border Security Force Detects Drone Intrusion Along Punjab Border, Recovers Contraband
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) detected and intercepted a drone intrusion attempt along the international border in Punjab.
• Following a search operation, BSF personnel recovered narcotics and a weapon package dropped by the drone.
• This incident highlights the persistent challenge of cross-border smuggling and the evolving tactics used by illicit networks.
• BSF has intensified surveillance and deployed advanced anti-drone technologies to counter such threats.
• The use of drones for smuggling poses significant internal security challenges, particularly in border states.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Punjab shares an international border with Pakistan.
Drones are increasingly used for smuggling arms, narcotics, and counterfeit currency.
Anti-drone technology includes jammers, net guns, and laser-based systems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cross-border security threats, with a focus on drone technology, along India's western borders.
Analyze the challenges faced by border guarding forces like BSF in countering drone intrusions and illicit activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current technological solutions and operational strategies employed to enhance border surveillance and security.
Examine the linkages between cross-border smuggling, organized crime, and internal security challenges in states like Punjab.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including policy interventions and international cooperation, to mitigate drone-related threats to national security.
GS3.imageDescription: BSF personnel inspecting a recovered drone and contraband items near a border fence.
GS3.trivia: BSF is also deployed for anti-Naxal operations and internal security duties in addition to border guarding.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft Policy Framework for AI Governance in India
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a draft policy framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance in India, inviting public comments.
• The framework aims to promote responsible AI development, ensuring ethical deployment, data privacy, and accountability.
• It addresses key aspects such as data quality, algorithmic bias, transparency, and the socio-economic impact of AI.
• India seeks to harness AI's potential for economic growth and public service delivery while mitigating associated risks.
• This policy is crucial for positioning India as a global leader in AI innovation with a focus on 'AI for All'.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.
AI governance refers to the policies and frameworks for developing and deploying AI systems responsibly.
Key concerns in AI: bias, privacy, security, transparency, accountability.
India aims to leverage AI in sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind developing a comprehensive AI governance framework for India, considering its unique socio-economic context.
Analyze the key principles and challenges addressed in NITI Aayog's draft policy, such as algorithmic bias, data privacy, and ethical considerations.
Evaluate the potential impact of AI on employment, economic growth, and public service delivery in India.
Examine the role of government, industry, and civil society in ensuring responsible AI development and deployment.
Critically assess how India can position itself as a global leader in AI innovation while upholding ethical standards and promoting 'AI for All'.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected nodes representing artificial intelligence and governance concepts.
GS3.trivia: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015.

GS3.headline: IMF Global Economic Outlook: India's Growth Projection Revised Upwards
GS3.explainer:
• The International Monetary Fund (IMF) released its latest 'World Economic Outlook' report, revising India's growth projection upwards for the current fiscal year.
• The revision is attributed to robust domestic demand, strong public investment, and a resilient services sector.
• Despite global headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties, India is expected to remain one of the fastest-growing major economies.
• The report, however, cautioned about persistent inflation risks and the need for continued structural reforms.
• This positive outlook reinforces confidence in India's economic fundamentals and its potential to drive global growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effect of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: IMF is an international organization, headquartered in Washington, D.C., working to foster global monetary cooperation.
It publishes the 'World Economic Outlook' report periodically.
GDP (Gross Domestic Product) is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
Structural reforms aim to improve the functioning of the economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's upward revision in the IMF's growth projections, despite global economic challenges.
Discuss the implications of a strong Indian economy for global economic stability and trade.
Evaluate the persistent risks highlighted by the IMF, such as inflation and global geopolitical tensions, and their potential impact on India's growth trajectory.
Examine the role of government policies and structural reforms in sustaining India's economic momentum.
Critically assess India's potential to become a major engine of global growth and the challenges it needs to overcome.
GS3.imageDescription: IMF logo against a backdrop of global economic charts and graphs.
GS3.trivia: India is a founding member of the International Monetary Fund.

GS3.headline: Latest Forest Survey Report Highlights Increase in India's Mangrove Cover
GS3.explainer:
• The biennial India State of Forest Report (ISFR) indicates a significant increase in India's mangrove cover over the past two years.
• The report attributes this growth to sustained conservation efforts, afforestation programs, and community participation.
• Mangroves play a crucial role in protecting coastlines, supporting biodiversity, and acting as carbon sinks.
• West Bengal, Gujarat, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands continue to hold the largest mangrove areas.
• This positive trend underscores India's commitment to coastal ecosystem protection and climate resilience.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: ISFR is published biennially by the Forest Survey of India (FSI).
Mangroves are salt-tolerant plant communities found in tropical and subtropical intertidal zones.
They protect coastlines from erosion, storm surges, and tsunamis.
Sundarbans in West Bengal is the largest mangrove forest in the world.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of mangroves and their role in coastal protection and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increase in India's mangrove cover, including government policies and community initiatives.
Evaluate the challenges faced in mangrove conservation, such as encroachment, pollution, and climate change impacts.
Examine the socio-economic benefits derived from healthy mangrove ecosystems for local communities.
Critically assess India's strategies for coastal zone management and the importance of integrated approaches for mangrove protection.
GS3.imageDescription: Dense mangrove forest along a calm coastal waterway.
GS3.trivia: Mangroves are often called 'coastal sentinels' due to their role in protecting shorelines.
